name: Active Directory Database Dump Attempt
description: Detects attempts to dump the Active Directory database (ntds.dit), a critical file containing sensitive information like password hashes. Attackers often target this file to obtain credentials for lateral movement and privilege escalation.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The Windows Security event ID related to the detection (e.g., process creation event IDs).

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the suspicious activity occurred.

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the process involved in the dump attempt (e.g., NTDSDump.exe, powershell.exe, cmd.exe).

parameters.name: process_path
parameters.description: The full path to the executable involved in the dump attempt.

parameters.name: parent_process_name
parameters.description: The name of the parent process that initiated the suspicious activity.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: The full command line arguments, indicating the attempt to access or copy 'ntds.dit'.
